prompt:
A small LED indicator module lying on a wooden electronics bench: a 5 mm red LED mounted on a tiny board or header, with a 220 Ω resistor and two jumper leads pre-wired so it can plug directly into a breadboard. The LED and resistor are clearly visible and the jumper leads terminate in pin connectors. In the background, a breadboard circuit shows another copy of the module already plugged in and glowing, illustrating its use in quick prototypes.

Near-future solarpunk electronics aesthetic with warm lighting, organized tools, and a bit of greenery or a small solar panel nearby. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible text. The composition should clearly communicate that this is a pre-wired LED indicator module ready to drop into breadboard projects.
